title: College of Engineering Classroom Recording and Equipment Setup
body: The College of Engineering has 2 different types of class recording rooms.  Please follow the instructions for your classroom. A few things to remember and some general advice: - You will need to use the microphone as well as 'start'/'stop' the recorder via the AMX touch panel controls found on the podium - It will not capture anything written on the glassboards- I would suggest using the document camera for handwritten materials - Use the mouse or a stylus to direct attention when using a computer/laptop/ or the document camera (avoid laser pointers and hand gestures towards the screen) - Repeat in-room questions for online students since we can only capture your audio via the microphone 1.) Self-record Systems: Recordings in these rooms are are automated, and can be started and stopped by the instructor in the room. ECB 1003 (Tong) EH 1227 EH 1610 EH 1800 EH 2317 EH 2534 ME 1106 ME 1143 ME 1153 ME 1152 ME 1156 ME 1163 ME 1164 MSE 265 - Recordings in these rooms will need to be scheduled and added to the room system for them to work.  If they are not scheduled, you won't be able to record. - Please contact Engineering Media Services by emailing ems@engr.wisc.edu and provide the following information: Instructor name & contact information Course number and title Days and times class will need recording Room location (from above) See this document for instructions on using the self record system: https://kb.wisc.edu/engr/media/105341 2.) Web Collaboration Software, USB Microphone and Document Camera Rooms: If you need to setup for this system, please follow instructions here: https://kb.wisc.edu/engr/media/98913 - Recordings in these rooms are at your discretion. - You will need to provide a laptop for the recordings. A USB-C adapter may be necessary to connect the IPEVO document camera and/or wireless room audio to your device. Best Practices - Keep room lights at full intensity - Use the mouse or a stylus to direct attention when using a computer or document camera 6.8.0 - Repeat in-room questions (when applicable) for online students (because in most cases, there are no ambient room microphones) - If you experience any technical issues with the podium lecture capture or room control equipment, contact Engineering Media Services by calling (608) 890-3325 or emailing ems@engr.wisc.edu . - If you experience any technical issues with the podium computer, contact Computer-Aided Engineering by calling (608) 262-5349 or emailing helpdesk@cae.wisc.edu .
